ucs.title: [LLM] RTM .bit / Namecoin dead-drop resolver C2 from non-browser process
ucs.description: Hunts for the RTM banker's signature C2 channel: hardcoded dead-drop pages on Namecoin (.bit) TLD or known public .bit→IP resolvers (bit.nu, bit.dnshome.de, namecha.in, namecoin.id). RTM is one of the very few crews that still operates over .bit infrastructure, so any non-browser process resolving these names is high-fidelity for this group. Maps to ESET's RTM cluster (ESET RTM Feb 2017, Mar 2019).
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(DNS.dest) as resolver values(DNS.answer) as answer from datamodel=Network_Resolution.DNS where (DNS.query="*.bit" OR DNS.query="*.bit.nu" OR DNS.query="*.bit.dnshome.de" OR DNS.query="*namecha.in" OR DNS.query="*namecoin.id" OR DNS.query="*opennic.glue") by DNS.src DNS.query host
| `drop_dm_object_name(DNS)`
| rename src as src_ip
| join type=left src_ip [
    | tstats summariesonly=true values(Processes.process_name) as process_name values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name!="chrome.exe" Processes.process_name!="msedge.exe" Processes.process_name!="firefox.exe" Processes.process_name!="brave.exe" Processes.process_name!="opera.exe" by Processes.dest
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
    | rename dest as src_ip
  ]
| where isnotnull(process_name)
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
| table firstTime lastTime host src_ip user process_name process query answer count
ucs.kql:
// RTM .bit dead-drop C2 — Namecoin TLD or known public bit→IP resolver hosts
let _bit_resolver_hosts = dynamic([".bit.nu", ".bit.dnshome.de", ".bit.opennic.glue", "namecha.in", "namecoin.id", ".d.namecoin.webbtc.com"]);
let _allowed_browsers = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","iexplore.exe"]);
let NetHits = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where isnotempty(RemoteUrl)
    | where RemoteUrl matches regex @"(?i)(^|\.)[a-z0-9-]+\.bit($|[/:?])"
         or RemoteUrl has_any (_bit_resolver_hosts)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_allowed_browsers)
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, DeviceId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
              InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessSHA256,
              InitiatingProcessAccountName;
let DnsHits = DeviceEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType == "DnsQueryResponse"
    | extend Q = tolower(tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).QueryName))
    | where Q endswith ".bit" or Q has_any (_bit_resolver_hosts)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_allowed_browsers)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, DeviceId, RemoteUrl = Q, RemoteIP = "", RemotePort = int(null),
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
              InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessSHA256 = tostring(InitiatingProcessSHA256),
              InitiatingProcessAccountName;
union isfuzzy=true NetHits, DnsHits
| summarize FirstSeen = min(Timestamp), LastSeen = max(Timestamp),
            HitCount = count(),
            Resolvers = make_set(RemoteUrl, 25),
            Cmds      = make_set(InitiatingProcessCommandLine, 10)
            by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessSHA256, InitiatingProcessAccountName
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// RTM .bit dead-drop C2 over Sentinel telemetry — Windows DNS server logs + ASIM network sessions
let _bit_resolver_hosts = dynamic([".bit.nu", ".bit.dnshome.de", ".bit.opennic.glue", "namecha.in", "namecoin.id", ".d.namecoin.webbtc.com"]);
let DnsHits = DnsEvents
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | extend Q = tolower(Name)
    | where Q endswith ".bit" or Q has_any (_bit_resolver_hosts)
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, ClientIP, Q, Source = "DnsEvents";
let WebHits = ImWebSession
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | extend H = tolower(coalesce(HttpHostName, tostring(parse_url(Url).Host)))
    | where H endswith ".bit" or H has_any (_bit_resolver_hosts)
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer = DvcHostname, ClientIP = SrcIpAddr, Q = H, Source = "ImWebSession";
let NetHits = ImNetworkSession
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | extend H = tolower(DstHostname)
    | where H endswith ".bit" or H has_any (_bit_resolver_hosts)
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer = DvcHostname, ClientIP = SrcIpAddr, Q = H, Source = "ImNetworkSession";
union isfuzzy=true DnsHits, WebHits, NetHits
| summarize FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated),
            HitCount = count(),
            Resolvers = make_set(Q, 25),
            Sources   = make_set(Source)
            by Computer, ClientIP
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: RTM Banker .bit Dead-Drop Resolver Lookup
id: a3f9e1c7-rtm-bit-c2-2026
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS resolution or HTTP traffic to Namecoin (.bit) TLD or known public .bit-to-IP resolver hosts. RTM banking trojan operators have used these dead-drop services since 2015 to publish C2 IPs.
references:
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2017/02/20/read-rtm-redaba-spies-pursuit-bank-payments-ru/
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2019/03/05/rtm-group-attacks-russian-banks/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1102.001
  - attack.t1071.004
  - actor.rtm
logsource:
  category: dns
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_bit:
    QueryName|endswith: '.bit'
  selection_resolvers:
    QueryName|contains:
      - '.bit.nu'
      - '.bit.dnshome.de'
      - '.bit.opennic.glue'
      - 'namecha.in'
      - 'namecoin.id'
      - '.d.namecoin.webbtc.com'
  condition: selection_bit or selection_resolvers
falsepositives:
  - Researchers / red-team intentionally probing Namecoin
  - OpenNIC users in research orgs
level: high
ucs.rationale: RTM is documented (ESET 2017/2019, Group-IB) as one of the only persistent users of Namecoin (.bit) infrastructure for C2 dead-drops, retrieving live C2 IPs through fixed public bit-to-IP resolver URLs. Mainstream Western enterprises virtually never resolve .bit names — combined with a non-browser initiating process this is single-event high-fidelity. Generic .bit detections would map to RTM, Necu
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] RTM LiteManager covert RAT install + Run-key persistence on banking-software host
ucs.description: Hunts the RTM crew's hands-on-keyboard step after the RTM downloader gains a foothold: silent deployment of LiteManager Free (ROMServer.exe / ROMFUSClient.exe) as a remote-access backdoor, dropped outside Program Files and registered under HKCU/HKLM Run keys. RTM specifically targets hosts running 1C-Buhgalteriya / iBank / RBS clients, so the detection scopes to devices with banking software present.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_path) as path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("ROMServer.exe","ROMFUSClient.exe","ROMService.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*ROMServer*","*ROMFUSClient*","*LiteManager*","*/silentinstall*","*-silentinstall*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| where NOT match(path, "(?i)C:\\\\Program Files( \\(x86\\))?\\\\LiteManager")
| join type=inner dest [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where (Registry.registry_path="*\\Run\\*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\RunOnce\\*") (Registry.registry_value_data="*ROMServer*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*ROMFUSClient*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*LiteManager*") by Registry.dest Registry.registry_path Registry.registry_value_name Registry.registry_value_data
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)`
  ]
| join type=left dest [
    | tstats summariesonly=true values(Processes.process_name) as banking_proc from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("1cv8.exe","1cv8c.exe","BSSClient.exe","iClBnk.exe","iBankClient.exe","RkClient.exe","clbank.exe") by Processes.dest
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
  ]
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
| table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name path parent cmdline registry_path registry_value_data banking_proc count
ucs.kql:
// RTM LiteManager covert deployment + Run-key persistence on hosts that also run RU banking clients
let LiteBins = dynamic(["romserver.exe","romfusclient.exe","romservice.exe","romviewer.exe"]);
let BankingProcs = dynamic(["1cv8.exe","1cv8c.exe","bssclient.exe","iclbnk.exe","ibankclient.exe","rkclient.exe","clbank.exe","sberbank.exe"]);
// 1) hosts where RU banking software actually runs (RTM's victimology)
let BankingHosts = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where tolower(FileName) in (BankingProcs)
    | summarize by DeviceId, DeviceName;
// 2) LiteManager binaries executed from a non-default path or with silent-install flags
let LiteDeploy = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where tolower(FileName) in (LiteBins)
         or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("ROMServer","ROMFUSClient","LiteManager","/silentinstall","-silentinstall","/password ","-password ")
    | where not(FolderPath matches regex @"(?i)^C:\\Program Files( \(x86\))?\\LiteManager")
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
              ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessSHA256;
// 3) Run / RunOnce / IFEO autorun referencing LiteManager
let LitePersist = DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has_any (@"\Run", @"\RunOnce", @"\Image File Execution Options", @"\Services")
    | where tolower(RegistryValueData) has_any ("romserver","romfusclient","litemanager","romservice")
    | project PersistTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData,
              PersistInitiator = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              PersistInitiatorCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
LiteDeploy
| join kind=inner LitePersist on DeviceId
| where PersistTime between (Timestamp - 1h .. Timestamp + 24h)
| join kind=inner BankingHosts on DeviceId
| project Timestamp, PersistTime, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
          ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData, PersistInitiator
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// RTM LiteManager covert deployment — Sentinel via SecurityEvent 4688 + WindowsEvent registry
let LiteBinsRx = @"(?i)\\(romserver|romfusclient|romservice|romviewer)\.exe$";
let BankingRx  = @"(?i)\\(1cv8c?|bssclient|iclbnk|ibankclient|rkclient|clbank|sberbank)\.exe$";
let BankingHosts = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName matches regex BankingRx
    | summarize by Computer;
let LiteDeploy = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where (NewProcessName matches regex LiteBinsRx)
         or (CommandLine has_any ("ROMServer","ROMFUSClient","LiteManager","/silentinstall","-silentinstall"))
    | where not(NewProcessName matches regex @"(?i)^C:\\Program Files( \(x86\))?\\LiteManager\\")
    | project DeployTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
              ParentProcessName, SubjectUserName;
let LitePersist = ImRegistryEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has_any (@"\Run", @"\RunOnce", @"\Image File Execution Options", @"\Services")
    | where tolower(tostring(RegistryValueData)) has_any ("romserver","romfusclient","litemanager","romservice")
    | project PersistTime = TimeGenerated, Computer = DvcHostname, RegistryKey, RegistryValueData,
              PersistInitiator = ActingProcessName;
LiteDeploy
| join kind=inner LitePersist on Computer
| where PersistTime between (DeployTime - 1h .. DeployTime + 24h)
| join kind=inner BankingHosts on Computer
| project DeployTime, PersistTime, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName,
          RegistryKey, RegistryValueData, PersistInitiator
| order by DeployTime desc
ucs.rationale: RTM (G0048) has a documented multi-year tradecraft of dropping LiteManager Free (rebranded as ROMServer/ROMFUSClient) as a stealth RAT specifically on Russian-language banking endpoints — ESET (2017/2019) and Group-IB call this out as one of RTM's signature behaviours, distinct from clusters like Buhtrap or Cobalt Group which favour Cobalt Strike / Metasploit. Scoping the join to hosts that also e
